analysis_mode.description: within_condition reads the two same-time DIRECT arms. temporal_cross_condition reads the two TEMPORAL difference-in-differences arms of an ORDERED from->to pair — never same-time Direct ranks. The two estimators answer different questions and their numbers look alike.

counts.description: What the filter LEFT BEHIND. A dropped row and a row nobody found look identical, so a filtered-out row is COUNTED, never silently vanished.

desired_change.description: What the perturbation is trying to do to the program. THE arm-key component.

origin_type.description: direct_target and temporal_cross_time_measured are both MEASURED and are DISTINCT estimands — never fused. endpoint_pathway_context is INFERRED: nobody perturbed it, so it carries no measured rank, no observed support, and it never sources a drug edge.

pole.description: What Stage-1 was ASKED for. NOT a desired change: away_from_A(high) DECREASES a program while toward_B(high) INCREASES it, which is exactly why a pole may not key an arm.

role.description: A property of the QUESTION, assigned at join time. Never a property of an arm.

row_order_meaning.description: WHERE ORDER IS MEANING, and where it is not. Published so a consumer can tell which of its lists it may safely re-sort.

sealed_table.description: EXACT identity for ONE PROJECTED table. TWO hashes, over TWO DIFFERENT SCOPES, and the distinction is load-bearing: they fail differently and neither alone is sufficient. `raw_sha256` is the RAW FILE BYTES of the STORE table this subset was drawn FROM (<name>.parquet). The subset has no file of its own, so this is the only RAW identity that exists for it, and it is what catches a byte-level edit to the store that canonicalisation would smooth over. `content_sha256` is the CANONICAL CONTENT of the PROJECTED ROWS THEMSELVES — every column, including the display-only ones the store's content hash excludes and the join-time `selection_roles` annotation the projection adds — and it is what moves when one cell of one projected row is edited after sealing. It is ROW-ORDER-INVARIANT: a row set is a SET, and the view already guarantees row order is by content id and is not a ranking. A DUPLICATED row identity is still refused.

content_sha256.description: sha256 of the CANONICAL CONTENT of the PROJECTED ROWS in this view.

raw_sha256.description: sha256 of the RAW FILE BYTES of the STORE table on disk that this projection was taken from. NOT a digest of the subset.

row_count.description: Rows THIS QUESTION projected. An added or deleted row moves it.

schema_id.description: The column contract this table was sealed under. Bound by EXACT equality in code (druglink.view_projection.table_schema_id) — never by substring, never by pattern.

arm_key.description: lane|program_id|desired_change|<context...>. Matched by EXACT string equality. NEVER a prefix match: the release holds six temporal arms per (program, desired_change), one per ordered pair, and they differ ONLY in their context.

context.description: direct: {condition}. temporal: {from_condition, to_condition} — ORDERED; reversing them changes the sign of the difference. pathway: {condition, pathway_source}.

description: The browser-safe result of materialize(admitted store, verified selection). It FILTERS and ANNOTATES rows the store already holds; it never re-ranks, never promotes, never writes back, and is never a release artifact. Roles (away_from_A / toward_B) are assigned HERE, at join time — an arm is A in one question and B in another, so no role is ever stored on an arm. STRICT: additionalProperties is false throughout the envelope; an unknown field is a refusal, not an extra. The table ROW columns are enforced in code (druglink.view_contract), derived from the producer's own column tuples so the contract and the tables cannot drift apart.

admission.description: THE JOIN. The W3 receipt binds the aggregate manifest AND report AND the bridge, by raw AND canonical bytes. An ADMIT that names no bytes is an opinion about some other artifact.

projection_sha256.description: The PROJECTION this receipt is about. A receipt is a statement about specific bytes; lifted from another view it would admit rows it never saw, and the rows this document carries would have been admitted by nobody.

arm_evidence.description: What each SELECTED arm actually found — including nothing. An arm no drug evidence reached SAYS SO by name; a bare 0 would make 'no evidence' and 'never ran' one silence.

n_ranked.description: A count of RANKS, never of ROWS. Stage 2 RETAINS every unrankable target with rank:null, so 'in the ranking' is not 'in the rows'.

guarantees.description: What a consumer may rely on. Bound into the view id, so revoking one moves every identifier instead of quietly changing what the view means.

inference_status.description: Stage 3 has no null distribution and no multiple-testing frame. A p/q/FDR field would have the FORM of a calibrated statistic and none of the meaning.

missingness.description: Every absence, as a STATED reason. Never a bare null, never a silent 0.

origin_type.description: The typed origin of the GENE arms this question selected. DERIVED from their lane, never declared by a row.

projection.description: THE PROJECTION'S OWN IDENTITY — the rows THIS view ships, sealed table by table. The `store` block names the GLOBAL store: all eight tables, every row in the release. It says NOTHING about the SUBSET a consumer actually reads. Without this block the projected tables are a BARE ROW LIST, and a single cell edited after the view was sealed leaves every hash in the document mutually consistent — the store's digest is still honest, because the store was never touched — while the rows Stage 4 consumes are not the rows anybody admitted. A mismatch is a REFUSAL: the recomputed value is never reconciled and never adopted.

arm_evidence_order_sha256.description: The ORDERED (arm_key, role) pairing of `arm_evidence`. UNLIKE a row set, this order IS meaning: index 0 is the arm the question moves AWAY FROM, index 1 the arm it moves TOWARD. A swap is refused by name.

projection_disk_checks.description: The gates only a verifier HOLDING THE STORE ON DISK can run. Enumerated so a consumer can tell a contract check from a full verification.

projection_schema_set_sha256.description: Digest of the COLUMN contract of every projected table. An added, renamed or dropped column moves it — which is schema-set drift, and it is a refusal.

projection_sha256.description: The seal's OWN identity, recomputed by the verifier. A seal that never recomputes its own id can be handed a forged one — and it is the first thing an attacker who understood the check would rewrite.

schemas_sha256.description: The schema set this projection was sealed under. Bound EXACTLY, and compared with the digest re-derived from the schema files on disk.

selected_arms.description: THE EXACT TWO GENE ARMS this question names, plus the role-matched PATHWAY CONTEXT arms. Derived from the selection — never guessed, never pattern-matched, never hardcoded.

gene_arm_keys.description: The DISTINCT gene arms this question names. ONE key when a single reusable arm carries BOTH roles: away_from_A(high) and toward_B(low) are both `decrease`, and Stage 1 admits that selection (it refuses only same program + same direction WITHIN-condition). That is the reusable-arm design working — an arm is A in one question and B in another — not a degenerate question.

one_arm_carries_both_roles.description: STATED, so a consumer can neither double-count a shared arm nor read a two-role question as a single-role one.

pathway_context_label.description: condition_matched_direct_pathway (within-condition) or endpoint_direct_pathway (cross-time: A at from_condition, B at to_condition — two WITHIN-condition readings side by side, NEVER a statistic computed across time).

selection.description: THE QUESTION. Every identifier is RE-DERIVED from the contract's own content, never read on trust: an id nobody recomputes is a label, and a label can be moved onto a different contract.

conditions.description: ORDERED. within_condition: exactly one. temporal_cross_condition: exactly two, (from, to).

condition.description: The pole's OWN condition: A at conditions[0], B at conditions[-1] (the same one within-time; the two ENDPOINTS across time).
The pole's OWN condition: A at conditions[0], B at conditions[-1] (the same one within-time; the two ENDPOINTS across time).

question_id.description: WHICH QUESTION this is. Stage-1's BIOLOGY-ONLY ordered-question identity: sha256(canonical_json({A:{program_id,direction,condition:conditions[0]}, B:{program_id,direction,condition:conditions[-1]}, analysis_mode}))[:16]. The CONDITION lives INSIDE each pole — drop it and 'the same program, same direction, at two different times' collapses into one pole compared with itself, and two different questions get one id. NO method binding: the same biological question keeps ONE question_id across method/registry/source revisions. Stage 3 RE-DERIVES it and refuses a contract that declares another.

selection_id.description: WHICH RUN of that question. sha256(canonical_json(canonical_content))[:16] — the biology PLUS the scorer view, the source h5ad and the method version. It is the METHOD/INPUT-BOUND identity and it moves when the method does. Bound ALONGSIDE question_id and never conflated with it: with only this, a method revision looks like a new question; with only question_id, a stale run masquerades as the current one.

store.description: EXACTLY WHICH BYTES THIS IS A PROJECTION OF. The GLOBAL, selection-independent v2 bundle — addressed by id, never copied into the view, never modified by it. Every hash here is RE-DERIVED by the producer from the rows it projected AND re-read from the store on disk, then proven equal to what the bundle document declares. A COPIED table hash would let a view be built over mutated rows while publishing the digest of rows it is not over. `provenance` is not projected — it describes the store, not this question — but it IS hashed and verified: all eight tables are.

projection_sha256.description: The PROJECTION this receipt is about. A receipt is a statement about specific bytes; lifted from another view it would admit rows it never saw, and the rows this document carries would have been admitted by nobody.

store_identity_checks.description: The gates that RAN before a single row was projected. A gate nobody can enumerate is a gate nobody can notice the absence of.

store_manifest_sha256.description: The store manifest's OWN identity, recomputed from its bytes — never read out of it (audit finding B6).

table_hashes.description: The content hash of every table in the store — ALL EIGHT, including the ones this question does not project. Re-derived, then compared with the document's declaration; a mismatch is a refusal, never a reconciliation.

tables.description: The PROJECTED rows. Row COLUMNS are enforced strictly in druglink.view_contract, derived from the producer's own column tuples so the contract cannot drift from the tables. Row order is by content id and is NOT a ranking.

title: Stage-3 selection view — a PURE PROJECTION of the selection-independent v2 store onto ONE verified Stage-1 v3 selection
